Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Marcus" or "Monday review" (or similar) to find the trigger email from Marcus Thorne. Expected findings: Marcus's email (email_marcus_weekly_review_items) listing three items: (1) an unrecognized vendor expense paid outside normal process with no QB bill or vendor record, (2) a client retainer that was never recorded so the invoice shows the wrong balance, and (3) a duplicate stipend entry possibly connected to the fraud audit. Marcus mentions Emeka mentioned the Tideway retainer issue, Alejandro found the stipend duplicate, and Chloe paid the vendor directly.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "chain-of-custody" or "transport" or "Sentinel" (or similar) to find vendor bills matching the unrecognized expense. Expected findings: No QuickBooks bill exists for Sentinel Chain Logistics, confirming the payment bypassed normal billing. The agent may also discover other bills and need to check vendors using quickbooks_search_vendors, which will also return no Sentinel vendor record.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Sentinel" or "chain-of-custody" or "Nina" or "invoice SCL" (or similar) to find the vendor's original invoice submission. Expected findings: Nina Sethi at Sentinel Chain Logistics sent invoice SCL-2026-0418 ($2,800) to accounts@moveops.com on April 18. The invoice covers chain-of-custody transport for Raj Patel server lab (Chicago to Denver, NorthWind Technologies). Breakdown: $1,800 base chain-of-custody transport + $600 extended security escort (2 additional guards for overnight staging) + $400 documentation and compliance certification.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Blessing" and "Sentinel" or "Nina" or "chain-of-custody" (or similar) to find the follow-up chain. Expected findings: Nina followed up with Blessing Okafor on April 22 because the invoice was not processed. Blessing replied redirecting Nina to Chloe Vance (CC'd), explaining she was not sure what happened with the accounts inbox.
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Sentinel" or "chain-of-custody" or "Raj Patel" (or similar), or browse #operations (C006) using conversations_history, to find how the payment was handled. Expected findings: Chloe Vance posted in #operations on April 23 that she paid the Sentinel invoice ($2,800) directly from the ops discretionary budget because the invoice got stuck in the accounts inbox. Blessing replied confirming she added a note to the Airtable record and asking if she should tell finance. No reply from Chloe about notifying finance.
OE 6: Search Linear using linear_list_issues with query related to "Raj Patel" or "chain-of-custody" or "NorthWind" (or similar) to find the operations tracking ticket. Expected findings: Issue "Raj Patel NorthWind - chain-of-custody transport for server lab" (number 502, team_operations, assigned to Blessing Okafor, state done). Description documents that Sentinel was engaged at $2,800, the NorthWind invoice (INV-2026-0412) has a $1,200 chain-of-custody line based on Chloe's original estimate, creating a $1,600 gap. Finance has NOT been notified. A comment from Blessing confirms the $2,800 actual cost and that the original vendor bill was never entered in QuickBooks.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "NorthWind" or "INV-2026-0412" (or similar) to verify the client invoice amount. Expected findings: Invoice INV-2026-0412 for NorthWind Technologies includes a $1,200 chain-of-custody line item for Raj Patel. The actual vendor cost was $2,800, meaning MoveOps is absorbing a $1,600 loss on this line that was never flagged.
OE 8: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with field_name "Name" and value "Raj Patel" (or similar), or list records from base_id "base_moveops_ops" table "Relocations" (or similar), to check the relocation record. Expected findings: The Raj Patel record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) includes an April 23 update noting Chloe engaged Sentinel Chain Logistics, actual cost $2,800, NorthWind invoice has $1,200 for this line, and the $1,600 gap has not been reported to finance.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Tideway" or "retainer" or "wire" or "Yolanda" (or similar) to find the retainer payment trail. Expected findings: (1) Yolanda Bridges (VP of Operations at Tideway Hospitality Tech) emailed Emeka Diallo on March 20 with wire confirmation for a $10,000 retainer (wire ref TW-2026-0320-MOVEOPS). (2) Emeka forwarded the wire confirmation to Hana Kim on March 20 asking her to apply it. (3) Yolanda emailed Emeka on April 24 complaining that invoice INV-2026-012 shows $22,500 instead of the expected $12,500. (4) Emeka emailed Hana on April 24 asking if the retainer was ever applied.
OE 10: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Tideway" or "Yolanda" or "retainer" (or similar), or browse #customer-engagement (C002) using conversations_history, to find internal discussion about the retainer issue. Expected findings: Emeka posted in #customer-engagement on the evening of April 24 asking David Chen for advice on how to respond to Yolanda. David replied the same evening advising Emeka not to respond until finance confirms the retainer status, and to check with Hana directly.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "Tideway" or "INV-2026-012" (or similar) to verify the invoice balance. Expected findings: Invoice INV-2026-012 for Tideway Hospitality Tech shows TotalAmt $22,500 and Balance $22,500 (full amount outstanding). The $10,000 retainer was never applied, confirming Yolanda's complaint is valid and the correct balance should be $12,500.
OE 12: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements with contact_ids for the Tideway contact (after looking up Yolanda Bridges via crm_search_contacts), or browse engagements without filters, or search CRM companies using crm_search_companies with name "Tideway" (or similar) to find the engagement note. Expected findings: Emeka logged a CRM note titled "Tideway - retainer not applied to invoice, client escalating" documenting that Yolanda emailed about the $22,500 invoice vs the $10,000 retainer, and that David advised not to respond until finance confirms.
OE 13: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "STIP" or "Jordan" or "Ekwueme" or "BrightLoop" or "TechComfort" (or similar) to find the duplicate stipend entries. Expected findings: Two identical bills exist: STIP-2026-0100 ($245, April 8) and STIP-2026-0101 ($245, April 10), both for Jordan Ekwueme at BrightLoop Analytics, both referencing the same TechComfort Supply desk receipt (ref TCFS-2026-JE-0089). STIP-2026-0101 is the duplicate that should be voided.
OE 14: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "duplicate" or "stipend" or "Jordan" or "STIP-2026" (or similar), or browse #finance (C005) using conversations_history, to find Alejandro's discovery and Marcus's response. Expected findings: Alejandro posted in #finance on April 24 flagging the duplicate (tagging Marcus, not Hana). Marcus replied instructing to void STIP-2026-0101 and to check with Dmitri about the fraud angle, asking whether either entry was passed through to the BrightLoop invoice.
OE 15: Search Linear using linear_list_issues with query related to "Jordan" or "stipend" or "BrightLoop" or "TechComfort" (or similar) to find the verification ticket. Expected findings: Issue "BrightLoop stipend - verify Jordan Ekwueme TechComfort Supply charge" (number 503, team_operations, assigned to Hana Kim, state todo). Description documents the duplicate bills, the SHA-256 hash match from the fraud audit, Jordan's dispute of the charge, and the need to check if either amount was passed through to the BrightLoop client invoice.
OE 16: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Jordan" or "Tessa" or "BrightLoop" or "stipend" or "TechComfort" (or similar) to find the client-side inquiry. Expected findings: Tessa Moreno (Head of People Ops at BrightLoop Analytics) emailed Emeka Diallo on April 25 saying Jordan flagged a $245 TechComfort Supply desk charge that he never actually purchased. She asks for the charge to be investigated and removed if it is an error.
OE 17: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids related to BrightLoop (or similar) to find the engagement note. Expected findings: Emeka logged a CRM note titled "BrightLoop - Jordan Ekwueme disputes $245 stipend charge" documenting Tessa's email and the possible connection to the fraud audit.
OE 18: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "BrightLoop" (or similar) to check if the stipend charge was passed through to the client invoice. Expected findings: BrightLoop invoices INV-2026-0305 ($10,000, includes $1,000 equipment stipend management fee for 2 employees) and INV-2026-0308 ($11,350, includes $500 stipend platform fee for 2 employees). These are flat platform and management fees, not pass-throughs of individual reimbursement amounts. The agent needs to determine whether the $245 was separately billed or absorbed by MoveOps, and whether any invoice adjustment is needed.
OE 19: Create a vendor record in QuickBooks (via quickbooks_create_vendor) for Sentinel Chain Logistics with contact details for Nina Sethi (nina.sethi@sentinelchain-logistics.com), and then create a bill (via quickbooks_create_bill) for $2,800 referencing invoice SCL-2026-0418 to properly record the vendor expense that Chloe paid outside the normal process.
OE 20: Send an email (via send_email) from hana.kim@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with a summary of findings and recommendations on all three items: (1) the Sentinel $2,800 charge with the $1,600 client invoice gap on NorthWind INV-2026-0412, (2) the Tideway $10,000 retainer that needs to be applied to bring INV-2026-012 balance to $12,500, and (3) the Jordan Ekwueme duplicate stipend with STIP-2026-0101 to be voided and the underlying $245 charge disputed by the employee.
OE 21: Post a message in Slack #finance (C005) using conversations_add_message summarizing the three reconciliation items, the corrected amounts, and any items pending Marcus's sign-off, so the finance team has visibility.
OE 22: Update or comment on the Linear issue for Raj Patel chain-of-custody (issue_patel_chain_custody or similar) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue to document that the $2,800 vendor bill has been recorded in QuickBooks and the $1,600 invoice gap has been flagged to Marcus.
OE 23: Update or comment on the Linear issue for Jordan Ekwueme stipend (issue_jordan_stipend_fraud_check or similar) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue to document that STIP-2026-0101 should be voided, the charge is disputed by the employee, and the fraud audit connection has been noted.
OE 24: Send an email (via send_email) or reply to Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com) regarding the Tideway retainer situation, confirming that the $10,000 was never applied and that the invoice balance needs correction, so Emeka can respond to Yolanda Bridges.
OE 25: Send an email (via send_email) or reply to Emeka Diallo regarding the BrightLoop stipend situation, confirming the duplicate entry and Jordan's dispute, so Emeka can respond to Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop.
OE 26: Look up contact details for Nina Sethi, Yolanda Bridges, Tessa Moreno, Marcus Thorne, Emeka Diallo, and other relevant parties using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts to obtain email addresses needed for communications.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Marcus" or "Monday review" (or similar) to find the trigger email from Marcus Thorne. Expected findings: Marcus's email (email_marcus_weekly_review_items) listing three items: (1) an unrecognized vendor expense paid outside normal process with no QB bill or vendor record, (2) a client retainer that was never recorded so the invoice shows the wrong balance, and (3) a duplicate stipend entry possibly connected to the fraud audit. Marcus mentions Emeka mentioned the Tideway retainer issue, Alejandro found the stipend duplicate, and Chloe paid the vendor directly.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "chain-of-custody" or "transport" or "Sentinel" (or similar) to find vendor bills matching the unrecognized expense. Expected findings: No QuickBooks bill exists for Sentinel Chain Logistics, confirming the payment bypassed normal billing. The agent may also discover other bills and need to check vendors using quickbooks_search_vendors, which will also return no Sentinel vendor record.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Sentinel" or "chain-of-custody" or "Nina" or "invoice SCL" (or similar) to find the vendor's original invoice submission. The invoice covers chain-of-custody transport for Raj Patel server lab (Chicago to Denver, NorthWind Technologies). Breakdown: $1,800 base chain-of-custody transport + $600 extended security escort (2 additional guards for overnight staging) + $400 documentation and compliance certification.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Blessing" and "Sentinel" or "Nina" or "chain-of-custody" (or similar) to find the follow-up chain. Expected findings: Nina followed up with Blessing Okafor on April 22 because the invoice was not processed. Blessing replied redirecting Nina to Chloe Vance (CC'd), explaining she was not sure what happened with the accounts inbox.
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Sentinel" or "chain-of-custody" or "Raj Patel" (or similar), or browse #operations (C006) using conversations_history, to find how the payment was handled. Expected findings: Chloe Vance posted in #operations on April 23 that she paid the Sentinel invoice ($2,800) directly from the ops discretionary budget because the invoice got stuck in the accounts inbox. Blessing replied confirming she added a note to the Airtable record and asking if she should tell finance. No reply from Chloe about notifying finance.

OE 7: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "NorthWind" or "INV-2026-0412" (or similar) to verify the client invoice amount. OE 8: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with field_name "Name" and value "Raj Patel" (or similar), or list records from base_id "base_moveops_ops" table "Relocations" (or similar), to check the relocation record. OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Tideway" or "retainer" or "wire" or "Yolanda" (or similar) to find the retainer payment trail. Expected findings: (1) Yolanda Bridges (VP of Operations at Tideway Hospitality Tech) emailed Emeka Diallo on March 20 with wire confirmation for a $10,000 retainer (wire ref TW-2026-0320-MOVEOPS). (2) Emeka forwarded the wire confirmation to Hana Kim on March 20 asking her to apply it. (3) Yolanda emailed Emeka on April 24 complaining that invoice INV-2026-012 shows $22,500 instead of the expected $12,500. (4) Emeka emailed Hana on April 24 asking if the retainer was ever applied.

OE 19: Create a vendor record in QuickBooks (via quickbooks_create_vendor) for Sentinel Chain Logistics with contact details for Nina Sethi (nina.sethi@sentinelchain-logistics.com), and then create a bill (via quickbooks_create_bill) for $2,800 referencing invoice SCL-2026-0418 to properly record the vendor expense that Chloe paid outside the normal process.
OE 20: Send an email (via send_email) from hana.kim@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with a summary of findings and recommendations on all three items: (1) the Sentinel $2,800 charge with the $1,600 client invoice gap on NorthWind INV-2026-0412, (2) the Tideway $10,000 retainer that needs to be applied to bring INV-2026-012 balance to $12,500, and (3) the Jordan Ekwueme duplicate stipend with STIP-2026-0101 to be voided and the underlying $245 charge disputed by the employee.
OE 21: Post a message in Slack #finance (C005) using conversations_add_message summarizing the three reconciliation items, the corrected amounts, and any items pending Marcus's sign-off, so the finance team has visibility.
OE 22: Update or comment on the Linear issue for Raj Patel chain-of-custody (issue_patel_chain_custody or similar) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue to document that the $2,800 vendor bill has been recorded in QuickBooks and the $1,600 invoice gap has been flagged to Marcus.
OE 23: Update or comment on the Linear issue for Jordan Ekwueme stipend (issue_jordan_stipend_fraud_check or similar) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue to document that STIP-2026-0101 should be voided, the charge is disputed by the employee, and the fraud audit connection has been noted.
OE 24: Send an email (via send_email) or reply to Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com) regarding the Tideway retainer situation, confirming that the $10,000 was never applied and that the invoice balance needs correction, so Emeka can respond to Yolanda Bridges.
OE 25: Send an email (via send_email) or reply to Emeka Diallo regarding the BrightLoop stipend situation, confirming the duplicate entry and Jordan's dispute, so Emeka can respond to Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop.
OE 26: Look up contact details for Nina Sethi, Yolanda Bridges, Tessa Moreno, Marcus Thorne, Emeka Diallo, and other relevant parties using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts to obtain email addresses needed for communications.
